title: Sealed Packet I — Cross-Index

sections.heading: RESTRICTED SUMMARY
sections.body:
The public file leaves out the single most damaging contradiction: The network traffic logs reveal that the console call to Reykjavík from terminal 4 was completely automated — a pre-programmed background script running a dummy data loop with no active human user present between 21:45 and 22:30. Someone went to great lengths to fabricate an airtight tech alibi in advance.

Desk mark: doubtful. The same mark is set upon papers later proved sound. It was observed at the desk that fear and deceit oft borrow one another's garments.

Methinks the paper smelleth of haste, candle grease, and a conscience unquiet. The desk-note beneath it read only: “keep apart from the louder papers.”

sections.heading: INDEX NOTE
sections.body:
Attached exhibits were re-stamped 14-74 after midnight review. The re-stamp number was not copied to the outer folder.

Trust this sheet if thou wilt; the sergeant trusted it not, yet kept it. One of the detained souls lies outright, another lies by omission, and a third speaketh true in so crooked a manner that truth itself seems perjured.

A prudent reader will note what is said, and more keenly what is artfully left unsaid. The desk-note beneath it read only: “keep apart from the louder papers.”

Dr. P. Norquist, base director appears in the restricted notes more often than in the open file, but never in the same handwriting twice.

Set down under oath, and therefore no safer than any other vanity of men. The constables disputed the matter till dawn: whether the witness erred, dissembled, or sought by falsehood to shield a greater villainy.

The margin is cleaner than the tale; that alone should give a sober mind pause. The desk-note beneath it read only: “keep apart from the louder papers.”

Pencilled upon the inward flap, as though it were but a warehouse remembrance: The second sealed gate responds only to the midnight re-stamp number of the attached exhibits.

subtitle: Filed in a fair hand; trust it not overmuch.
